data.title: As a developer or agent, ticket transition rejects deleted as a target status and treats deleted tickets as terminal when evaluating children
data.description:
Story: Prevent any path from transitioning a ticket to deleted except through ticket delete, and ensure all subsystems that check closed/terminal status treat deleted equivalently to closed.

Done Definitions:
- ticket transition <id> <current> deleted exits non-zero with error: 'deleted is not a valid transition target -- use ticket delete <id> to delete a ticket'
- Status validation in ticket-transition.sh explicitly lists deleted in a blocked-targets guard before the generic validation
- CLOSED_STATUSES in ticket-transition.sh Python inline block includes deleted
- CLOSED_STATUSES in ticket-unblock.py includes deleted
- sprint-next-batch.sh terminal status check includes deleted
- Unit tests: (a) transition to deleted returns error with instructional message, (b) CLOSED_STATUSES recognizes deleted in all three locations, (c) a parent with one deleted and one closed child passes the closure gate

Considerations:
- [Scope] Three independent CLOSED_STATUSES definitions exist (ticket-transition.sh Python inline, ticket-unblock.py, sprint-next-batch.sh) -- all three must be updated in a single story to avoid divergence windows
- [Maintainability] Consider whether a shared constant for CLOSED_STATUSES would prevent future divergence
- [Testing] The blocked-transition test must confirm the exact error message text matches what the instructional error in ticket-delete.sh tells the agent to expect
